title: atriplex glauca subsp. palaestina
intro: Atriplex glauca subsp. palaestina, also known as the shrubby orach, is a subspecies of the Atriplex glauca plant, which belongs to the amaranth family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Atriplex glauca subsp. palaestina are said to provide relief from respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues, offering several health benefits. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
